mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 3 months raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and signals continued Fed tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as valuations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.2% with FOMC expected to hike further in Dec 2018
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month FEDFUNDS change and 20d forward SPX returns is not negative and statistically significant (p>0.05) over rolling 3-year windows, or positive returns follow >60% of rate-rise episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE falls below 3.8%, labor market tightness raises wage inflation risk and hawkish Fed repricing, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price faster tightening.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=3.7% as of Nov 2018, near multi-decade low
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE < 3.8%, mean 5d forward SPX return is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.10) or is positive in over 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising FEDFUNDS combined with decelerating INDPRO growth signals monetary tightening occurring alongside weakening industrial activity, a stagflation-like configuration that compresses equity risk premia and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3m change > +0.25pp AND INDPRO 3m annualized growth < 1%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=109.39 as of Nov 2018 with growth decelerating
failure_condition: Joint trigger instances show 60d forward SPX returns not significantly more negative than unconditional baseline (difference in means p>0.10) across walk-forward folds.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, robust RSAFS growth signals resilient consumer demand that supports near-term corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; this relationship weakens or reverses once the cycle turns toward contraction.
failure_condition: During periods classified as late-cycle expansion, correlation between RSAFS 3m growth and 20d forward SPX return is not positive and significant (p>0.05) over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A positive trailing 6-month trend in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days via improved earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO 6m trend and 20d forward SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST exceeding 10% year-over-year signals a housing sector downturn that historically leads broader economic slowdowns, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as recession risk repricing occurs.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=1228 thousand units as of Oct 2018, down from 2018 peaks
failure_condition: Instances where HOUST YoY decline exceeds 10% show 60d forward SPX returns positive in more than 55% of cases, or mean return not significantly negative (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in PAYEMS growth and an uptick in UNRATE from its cycle low indicates a labor market inflection point that precedes broader economic weakening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3m avg change decelerates by >30% AND UNRATE rises >0.2pp from trailing 6m low
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=149893 thousand, UNRATE=3.7% as of Nov 2018
failure_condition: Joint trigger episodes do not produce 60d forward SPX returns significantly below unconditional mean (p>0.10) across at least two distinct historical cycles.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening regime, an acceleration in CPIAUCSL month-over-month inflation raises expectations of faster Fed rate hikes, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprice tightening risk; outside tightening regimes this effect is muted.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL MoM annualized > 2.5% while FEDFUNDS trend rising
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL=252.876 as of Nov 2018, FEDFUNDS=2.2% amid hiking cycle
failure_condition: In tightening-regime periods, 5d forward SPX returns following high CPI prints are not significantly negative (p>0.10), or effect disappears when regime filter is removed, indicating no regime dependence.
